flashcards.front: What is the result of A ∪ ∅?
flashcards.back: A ∪ ∅ = A (Union with empty set returns original set)
Elements in either set but not both: A Δ B = (A-B) ∪ (B-A)

flashcards.front: What data structure represents sets using binary flags?
flashcards.back: Bit vectors where each bit represents element membership

flashcards.front: How does hash table collision affect set operations?
flashcards.back: Collisions may lead to inaccurate membership tests without proper handling
Used in database joins to combine tables

flashcards.front: Time complexity of union in bit vectors
flashcards.back: O(n) where n is vector length, using bitwise OR
Probabilistic set representation for space-efficient membership tests
EXCEPT operator returns tuples in first query not in second
P(S) contains all subsets of S, including ∅ and S itself
High load factor increases collisions, affecting set operation accuracy
